verdict_reason: Blocking acceptance failures: the hardware decision document does not include the required full canonical pin SHA, and the task manifest/evidence referenced by the document is absent from the staged commit.

findings.description: The prep green criterion requires a reference to the canonical pin SHA. This line only says the pin was verified against docs/canonical_pin.txt, and the later table lists short per-file pin prefixes; the document never records canonical_pin_sha 5a5afbbaf12c01a4e04d2a710cdbdefebf4faf3ff709c113b3e172149d8878b2.

Universal U1/U2/U5/U7 require a task manifest with task_id, evidence_links, test_commands, and populated fields. git diff --cached --name-only stages only docs/hardware_decision.md, and no 0_6_hardware_decision_doc.json or 0.6.json manifest was found, so this is a prose-only doc update without the required manifest evidence.

The prep criterion requires the document to explicitly note physical procurement for .private/hardware_quote_*.pdf and tests/hardware/bakeoff_results.json is deferred to Phase 4 entry. The document defers tests/hardware/bakeoff_results.json, but marks .private/hardware_quote_*.pdf as not required and uses .private/ovh_quote_*.pdf on the Phase 4 procurement line instead of explicitly deferring the named artifact.

The document claims 8x H200 in OVH BHS has quoted availability, but the same document says procurement and quote artifacts are deferred. Without a committed quote or manifest evidence, this should be qualified as planned procurement or tied to a concrete evidence artifact.

The document pre-claims the decision artifact is signed via the Codex APPROVE gate during this orchestrator iteration. At review time the commit is still being prepared and this verdict is REQUEST_CHANGES, so the document should not assert completion of approval/signing before the gate actually passes.
